BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Archived historical break-even worksheet for Thompson's Hardware on Main Street, retained in the read-only tax history from Megan's part-time bookkeeping years. Revenue lines are stated as monthly gross margin by department.

CurrentState.Note: Historical figures from a prior operating period; retained for tax records only. The MembershipDues line represents hardware and tool counter sales margin, DropIns the paint and plumbing counter, EquipmentSales the garden and seasonal aisle, and the InstructorPay_Raj line the part-time counter staff payroll.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Main Street rent rises to $750/mo
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Total monthly operating expenses less the paint, plumbing, garden, and seasonal margin, divided by the $12 average per-transaction hardware margin.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Modest. Absorbed by current counter volume with a comfortable buffer.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Main Street rent rises to $850/mo
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still positive; a slightly stronger tool and paint month restores the prior margin.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even sits near 100 hardware transactions a month; current counter volume clears roughly $5,300/mo after inventory and operating costs.
ActionItems: Review vendor pricing at the next inventory reorder cycle
Reassess part-time counter staff hours against foot traffic
